highlights.title: Utilization
highlights.body: Weighted avg. utilization (throughput-based) reached 19.7% (-185 bps MoM, -58 bps vs. Budget). Anchored LTO: 22.8% (-375 bps MoM); Destination: 4.9% (-31 bps MoM).

highlights.title: VCN Revenue (ex. DAE)
highlights.body: Revenues (excl. VAT) increased +5.0% MoM, reaching MXN $10.4m. LTO / B2C / DiDi split: 58.6% / 39.4% / 2.0%.

highlights.title: Signed Hub - Presa Tepuxtepec (CDMX)
highlights.body: 15-year lease for a 1,758 m2 greenfield site on Av. Anillo Periferico. Capacity: 3.0 MW, 26 dual-connector DC chargers.

highlights.title: Watts App - User Growth
highlights.body: RAU +3.0% to 5,014. MAU +0.3% to 6,876, despite -5.0% decline in NAU.

highlights.title: Watts - Fraud Enforcement
highlights.body: Deployed fraud enforcement enabling blocking of users, cards, and devices linked to chargeback fraud.

lowlights.title: Pre-Development - Av. Colonia del Valle 615
lowlights.body: Negotiations fell through as the owner prioritized a competing self-storage proposal.

lowlights.title: Permitting Delays
lowlights.body: Several signed hubs facing delays: Merida 77, Mexico-Toluca, Mosqueta, and Vasconcelos.

lowlights.title: Watts - Session Flow Bug
lowlights.body: Malfunction in charging session close-out flow impacted ~3% of users (reduced to ~1.5% post-remediation).

lowlights.title: Watts - Login Disruption
lowlights.body: Login disruption last weekend of January affected ~0.5% of users (reduced to ~0.1% after corrective measures).

highlights.title: Utilization
highlights.body: Weighted avg. utilization reached 20.8% (+115 bps MoM, -2 bps vs. Budget). DAE chargers: 41.9% (+57 bps); Anchored LTO: 25.6% (+242 bps); Destination: 5.7% (+53 bps).

highlights.title: VCN Revenue (ex. DAE)
highlights.body: Revenues (excl. VAT) reached MXN $10.4m (-0.1% MoM; 3 fewer calendar days). Per-day basis: +8.7% vs. January. LTO / B2C / DiDi: 57.5% / 40.0% / 2.4%.

highlights.title: Signed Hub - Av. Adolfo Lopez Mateos 16 (CDMX)
highlights.body: 20-year lease, 1,279 m2 greenfield. Access to Anillo Periferico, Viaducto, Av. Constituyentes. Capacity: 2.0 MW, 19 DC 120 kW chargers.

highlights.title: Transvaal Hub - Construction Complete
highlights.body: Awaiting CFE energization. Operations began early March with our first BESS energy efficiency system.

highlights.title: Destination Sites
highlights.body: 5 new chargers signed (BYD EMSP Tijuana, Grupo Cleber Nuevo Leon). Total: 709 signed, 438 operational.

highlights.title: Dynamic Pricing MVP
highlights.body: Rolling out first Dynamic Pricing initiative; launch planned early April at Petrarca and Roma. Energy costs 14-48% lower vs. peak hours.

highlights.title: Watts App - User Growth
highlights.body: RAU +3.9% to 5,209; MAU +1.6% to 6,989, despite -4.4% decline in NAU.

lowlights.title: Pre-Development - Conquer
lowlights.body: Not pursued: 30-connector pure destination deal misaligned with asset type and location criteria.

lowlights.title: Pre-Development - Acosta Verde
lowlights.body: Host selected FAZT due to established long-term relationship. Our assessment identified 10+ locations failing minimum return thresholds.

lowlights.title: Destination Hubs - Commercial Delays
lowlights.body: Cost estimation bottlenecks from infrastructure team. Mitigating by prioritizing highest-value deals.

lowlights.title: Permitting Delays
lowlights.body: Ongoing delays for Merida 77, Mexico-Toluca, Mosqueta, and Rio Mississippi. Mitigating through government engagement (joint investment with Monterrey State Governor).

lowlights.title: Watts - iOS Login Bug
lowlights.body: Intermittent iOS login bug affected ~90 users/day over 10 days, causing delays up to 10 minutes.

highlights.title: Utilization
highlights.body: Weighted avg. utilization reached 20.0% (-84 bps MoM, +178 bps vs. Budget). DAE: 44.0% (+206 bps); Anchored LTO: 20.5% (-510 bps); Destination: 5.0% (-47 bps, +53 bps vs. Budget).

highlights.title: VCN Revenue (ex. DAE)
highlights.body: Revenues (excl. VAT) increased +21.9% MoM, reaching MXN $12.8m. LTO / B2C / DiDi: 55.5% / 41.4% / 3.1%.

highlights.title: Signed Hub - Eje 5 Norte (CDMX)
highlights.body: 15-year lease, 2,306 sqm greenfield on Av. Aquiles Serdan and Calzada Camarones. Capacity: 2.7 MW, 31 DC 120 kW chargers.

highlights.title: COD - Aeropuerto Hub (CDMX)
highlights.body: 21 chargers reached COD including our first BESS project (formerly Transvaal Hub).

highlights.title: COD - Las Aguilas Hub (CDMX)
highlights.body: 25 chargers reached COD; 2 additional chargers via optimized site layout (formerly Centenario Hub).

highlights.title: Destination Sites
highlights.body: 5 new chargers signed. Total: 686 signed, 465 operational, 221 pending installation.

highlights.title: Watts App - User Growth
highlights.body: RAU +13.6% to 5,921; MAU +16.6% to 8,150, driven by +25% increase in NAU.

lowlights.title: Pre-Development - Home Depot
lowlights.body: Unable to secure account; operations team declined dedicated parking spaces for charging.

lowlights.title: Commercial Delays
lowlights.body: Cost estimation bottlenecks remain structural. Prolonged delays may create relationship risk with certain hosts.

lowlights.title: Permitting Delays
lowlights.body: Ongoing delays for Merida 77, Mexico-Toluca, Mosqueta, Rio Mississippi. Parallel pre-construction process shortens critical path by 6-8 weeks.

lowlights.title: Competition - Orange Energy (DiDi subsidiary)
lowlights.body: Launching in Mexico: first CDMX hub (500 m2, 6 GBT chargers), ~MXN $9.28/kWh, waived activation fee. Asset-light model. Meeting scheduled April.

lowlights.title: Monterrey - Competitive Landscape
lowlights.body: Fazt leads with 9 sites at ~MXN $7.33/kWh. Strategy: lower capex/opex hub designs and cost-efficient locations.

lowlights.title: Watts - Infrastructure Constraints
lowlights.body: Onboarding 150+ connectors caused latency issues and two ~1-hour outages. Actions underway to ensure platform scalability.

highlights.title: Utilization
highlights.body: Weighted avg. utilization reached 17.6% (-242 bps MoM, +297 bps vs. Budget). DAE: 41.7% (-229 bps); Anchored LTO: 18.2% (-228 bps, +228 bps vs. Budget); Destination: 5.2% (+20 bps, +118 bps vs. Budget).

highlights.title: VCN Revenue (ex. DAE)
highlights.body: Revenues (excl. VAT) increased +2.3% MoM, reaching MXN $13.1m. LTO / B2C / DiDi: 55.0% / 42.7% / 2.3%.

highlights.title: Signed Hub - Coapa, Calzada del Hueso (CDMX)
highlights.body: 20-year lease, 2,349 sqm greenfield in southern CDMX. Capacity: 2.6 MW, 38 DC 120 kW chargers.

highlights.title: COD - Mixcoac Hub (CDMX)
highlights.body: 22 chargers reached COD; BESS energization expected early June (formerly Periferico Hub).

highlights.title: Dynamic Pricing - Early Results
highlights.body: 3 weeks into rollout at Aeropuerto, Petrarca, Roma. Off-peak throughput +4% to +27%; 11-24% of users migrating peak-to-off-peak; early habit formation confirmed.

highlights.title: Destination Sites
highlights.body: 8 new chargers signed. Total: 694 signed, 479 operational, 215 pending installation.

highlights.title: Watts App - User Growth
highlights.body: RAU +8.65% to 6,433; MAU +7.87% to 8,792, driven by +6% increase in NAU.

lowlights.title: Roma Hub - Temporary Closure
lowlights.body: Roma Anchored Hub temporarily closed by INVEA (land-use classification inconsistency). Demand redirected to Mixcoac (~6 km) and Petrarca. Reopening pursued through legal proceedings and Ministry of Economy support.

lowlights.title: Tijuana Market
lowlights.body: Challenging leasing: USD-denominated contracts, limited land availability, power constraints. Lost key lead to non-EV competitor. Engaging Colliers and strengthening local team.

lowlights.title: Pre-Development - Monumental 1882 (GDL)
lowlights.body: Advanced negotiations fell through as landlord shifted strategy from leasing to asset sale.

lowlights.title: Permitting Delays
lowlights.body: Ongoing delays for Carretera Mexico-Toluca, Merida 77, Rio Mississippi, Morones Prieto, Observatorio. Government engagement + streamlined pre-construction process (6-8 weeks faster).

lowlights.title: Destination Deployment
lowlights.body: 14 chargers installed vs. 38 on budget. Backlog: 215 pending installation. Institutional deals progressing slower than expected.

lowlights.title: El Globo
lowlights.body: Negotiations fell through (potential ride-hailing framework agreement) as landlord shifted focus to core business priorities.

lowlights.title: Watts - DiDi Discount Bug
lowlights.body: Bug in DiDi discount logic during Dynamic Pricing POC caused ~MXN $45K in excess discounts over 15 days.

highlights.title: Utilization
highlights.body: Weighted avg. utilization reached 17.0% (-57 bps MoM, +298 bps vs. Budget). DAE: 35.5% (-616 bps); Anchored LTO: 18.0% (-24 bps, +129 bps vs. Budget); Destination: 5.6% (+38 bps, +187 bps vs. Budget).

highlights.title: VCN Revenue (ex. DAE)
highlights.body: Revenues (excl. VAT) increased +6.9% MoM, reaching MXN $14.0m. LTO / B2C / DiDi: 54.3% / 43.6% / 2.1%.

highlights.title: Signed Hub - Tlalnepantla, Filiberto (CDMX)
highlights.body: 15-year lease, 2,190 sqm warehouse site. Planned: 3.8 MW, 32 dual-connector DC chargers.

highlights.title: Signed Hub - Manuel Acuna 2790 (GDL)
highlights.body: 18-year lease, 907 sqm greenfield. Planned: 2.7 MW, 15 dual-connector DC chargers.

highlights.title: Uber Charging Agreement / B2C Loyalty Program
highlights.body: Initiated discussions with Uber: (i) World Cup 8% charging discount; (ii) cashback loyalty program; (iii) long-term strategic partnership with hub integration and demand generation.

highlights.title: Dynamic Pricing - Maturation
highlights.body: 8 weeks into rollout. Off-peak session share: 27-30% (vs. 23-27% April). DP adopters: +69% revenue/user, +71% sessions/user vs. non-adopters. Repeat nocturnal users: 74.3% Aeropuerto, 73.7% Petrarca.

highlights.title: Watts App - User Growth
highlights.body: RAU +6.4% to 6,847; MAU +7.2% to 9,422, driven by +9% increase in NAU.

lowlights.title: Roma Hub - Ongoing Closure
lowlights.body: Roma Anchored Hub remains temporarily closed by INVEA. Reopening pursued through legal proceedings and Ministry of Economy support.

lowlights.title: Permitting Delays
lowlights.body: Ongoing delays for Merida 77, Rio Mississippi, Morones Prieto, Observatorio. Carretera Mexico-Toluca removed from risk list. Enhanced due diligence and intensified government engagement.

lowlights.title: Mixcoac Hub - Access & Governance
lowlights.body: Primary access obstructed by ancillary infrastructure introduced during development. Recovery underway; stricter cross-functional approval processes implemented.

lowlights.title: Destination Deployment
lowlights.body: Only 6 chargers installed vs. 37 on budget. Backlog: 223 pending installation. No destination deals signed in May.

lowlights.title: Torre Acacia (MTY)
lowlights.body: Potential termination notice issued following temporary uptime breaches from third-party internet provider. Connectivity resolved; discussions progressing favorably toward withdrawing the notice.

lowlights.title: Woolworth
lowlights.body: Advanced negotiation fell through due to lack of technical feasibility.

lowlights.title: Watts - AWS Payment Outage
lowlights.body: May 7: 45-minute payment downtime caused by AWS outage affecting our payment processor.
